_meta.SUPERSEDED_2026-07-24: This file originally documented RG-2 built off Titus 272RL (aero_supplyperf_aeroblade_2017.pdf), which is confirmed SUPPLY-ONLY data reused conservatively for a return application (no return-specific 272RL table exists). User later found a genuinely return-rated Titus cutsheet (350.355Rperf_300_350_2017.pdf, models 350R/350F/350R-SS explicitly labeled as Return-capable) and asked to rebuild RG-2 from that instead, since it represents an actual return product rather than a repurposed supply diffuser. THIS FILE NOW REFLECTS THE 350R-BASED REBUILD. See git history for the prior 272RL-based version if needed for reference.
_meta.source_cutsheet: 350.355Rperf_300_350_2017.pdf, page H37-H38 ("350R, 350F AND 350R-SS PERFORMANCE DATA"), user-supplied. Same PDF also contains 350ZR/350ZF (H39-40), 355R/355F (H41-42), and 355ZR/355ZF (H43-44) as distinct model variants with different NC/velocity characteristics -- user explicitly chose 350R/350F/350R-SS specifically, not the others.
_meta.no_deflection_correction_needed: Unlike 272RL (adjustable double-deflection blades, +7 NC correction for 45deg deflection), this cutsheet has no correction-factor table or deflection note at all -- it's a fixed-blade grille, NC values used directly, same as the Perforated Face return data used for RG-1.
_meta.dash_convention: NC < 10 (standard Titus convention, consistent with every other cutsheet used in this project)
_meta.target_nc: NC < 20, matching SD-4/SD-5 supply's target -- user's call once real return-rated data was available, superseding the earlier NC<15 discussion (which was specifically about compensating for reused supply data, no longer relevant now that this is real return data).
_meta.size_ceiling_decision: Capped at the '24x24'/'48x12' nominal size tier (core area 3.75 sq ft, max 1875 cfm at NC<20) -- user's explicit call to keep the same cap point as before rather than extend it as part of this rebuild. The pending 'increase RG-2 max CFM' request from earlier in the session is still open and unresolved, to be handled as a separate follow-up once a target number is given.
_meta.grouping_differs_from_272RL: This cutsheet's alternate-size groupings are NOT the same as 272RL's -- e.g. the 24x6/12x12 tier here is a 2-way group (no 18x8 member, unlike 272RL's 3-way 24x6/18x8/12x12 group), since 18x8 isn't a published nominal size in this product line at all. Every group in this cutsheet up to the cap is 2-way (one closest-to-square 'regular' pick, one most-elongated 'thin' pick) -- there was no need to exclude a 'middle' option the way some 272RL groups required.
_meta.architecture: Identical Regular/Thin/Custom system, unchanged by this rebuild: Auto Width/Auto Height (instance, Length) carry this breakpoint cascade; Duct Width/Duct Height (instance, Length, tied to actual geometry) fall back through Custom Width/Custom Height (if Custom Size type flag set + nonzero override) then Auto Width/Auto Height. Only the Auto Width/Auto Height formulas were changed by this rebuild.
_meta.verified: 28 boundary test cases (regenerate-and-read), expected values computed PROGRAMMATICALLY from the same breakpoint array rather than hand-typed, specifically to eliminate the repeated hand-indexing mistakes made earlier in this session when verifying the 272RL-based version. All 28 pass.

breakpoints_ascending_by_max_cfm.note: final/ceiling tier - also the unconditioned else clause in the formula
